data.title: Pentagon Considers Ending Partnership with AI Firm Anthropic Over Usage Restrictions
data.description:
The U.S. Pentagon is contemplating ending its partnership with Anthropic, an artificial intelligence company, due to the firm's insistence on imposing certain restrictions on military use of its models. According to Jin10, a senior government official disclosed that the Pentagon is urging four leading AI labs to allow military use of their tools for 'all legitimate purposes,' including sensitive areas such as weapons development, intelligence gathering, and battlefield operations. Anthropic has not agreed to these terms, and after months of difficult negotiations, the Pentagon has grown frustrated.

Anthropic maintains that two areas must remain prohibited: mass surveillance of U.S. citizens and fully autonomous weapon systems. The senior official noted that there is considerable ambiguity regarding what should fall into these categories and what should not. Moreover, negotiating each specific use case with Anthropic or dealing with Claude unexpectedly blocking certain applications is not feasible for the Pentagon.

The official stated that 'anything is possible,' including temporarily reducing collaboration with Anthropic or completely terminating the partnership. 'But if we believe this is the right course of action, we must find a suitable replacement for them,' the official added.

data.title: An ETH core contributor reveals the inside story of Tomasz's resignation: a long-standing power struggle has existed within the Ethereum Foundation.
data.description: Greg, a core contributor to the Ethereum consensus layer, revealed the inside story of Tomasz Stańczak's resignation as executive director of the Ethereum Foundation in an article published on the X platform. He stated that he was not surprised by this event because the Ethereum Foundation has long been plagued by power struggles. The organizational inertia of "this is how the Ethereum Foundation does things" makes reforms difficult to implement. Over the past year, key figure Vitalik Buterin has been largely absent, leading to problems such as inefficient resources and project stagnation within the foundation. The internal reforms initially promoted by Tomasz Stańczak were not widely accepted, which may have led to his departure. He possesses extensive industry experience, including backgrounds at Nethermind, Flashbots, and venture capital. Although the potential conflict of interest was disclosed in advance, some community members still have doubts. Furthermore, Tomasz Stańczak genuinely wanted to return to technical development, but he likely encountered resistance in pushing for reforms within the Ethereum Foundation, forcing him to leave. In the future, a leadership team with genuine business experience and decision-making ability is needed to ensure the organization's effective operation.

data.title: Wall Street Institutions Increase Investment in BitMine
data.description: BitMine Chair Tom Lee posted on X that several Tier-1 institutions have significantly increased their investments in BitMine during the same quarter. This development highlights growing interest from major financial entities in the cryptocurrency sector. The increased investment from Wall Street firms suggests a positive outlook for BitMine and potentially the broader crypto market. Lee's statement underscores the shifting dynamics in institutional investment strategies, as more traditional financial institutions explore opportunities within digital assets.

data.title: Bitcoin Basis Returns to Neutral Range as Premium Narrows
data.description:
Bitcoin's basis, measured by the 7-day simple moving average, has returned to a neutral range as the premium between spot and futures prices has significantly narrowed. According to ChainCatcher, this shift indicates a weakening demand for establishing long positions through derivatives, suggesting that the market is no longer pricing in aggressive risk appetite scenarios.

Typically, this scenario implies a decline in risk appetite, the beginning of a deleveraging process, and investors adopting a wait-and-see approach. Analyst Axel Adler Jr notes that the derivatives market is currently unable to continue driving prices upward. For bulls to regain momentum, stronger support from spot buying is necessary.

data.title: Senior Bankers Depart Raine Group to Establish New Firm in India
data.description: Three senior bankers from Raine Group in India, including the country head, are planning to leave the boutique advisory firm to start a new investment banking venture. Bloomberg posted on X, revealing that these departures mark a significant shift in the firm's Indian operations. The move comes as the bankers aim to leverage their experience to create a new entity in the competitive investment banking sector. Details about the new firm's focus and strategy have not been disclosed yet. The Raine Group, known for its advisory services in media, technology, and telecommunications, will need to adjust to these changes in its leadership team.

data.title: Sui Executives Predict 2025 as Key Year for Blockchain Adoption
data.description: Sui executives Evan Cheng and Stephen Mackintosh have identified 2025 as a crucial year for the institutional adoption of blockchain technology. According to NS3.AI, they highlighted tokenization and agentic commerce as significant growth areas. The announcement underscores the rising institutional interest and the evolving landscape of blockchain applications.

data.title: High-Profile Kidnapping in Arizona Sparks National Attention
data.description:
A kidnapping case in Tucson, Arizona, involving Nancy Guthrie, the mother of well-known morning show host Savannah Guthrie, has drawn nationwide attention. According to BlockBeats, Nancy Guthrie disappeared from her home on February 1, with police suspecting she was forcibly taken.

The incident quickly became a focal point for media and online sleuths, turning Tucson into a center of global interest. Authorities have yet to identify any suspects, and the investigation is ongoing.

Reports suggest the kidnappers demanded a ransom in Bitcoin, highlighting a new trend of using cryptocurrencies for extortion. A former FBI negotiation expert noted that despite the suspect appearing masked on surveillance footage, the intense scrutiny makes it difficult for those involved to remain hidden for long.

The White House has stated that the FBI will dedicate all resources to assist in the investigation. As the case continues to unfold, the local community has shown support by displaying yellow ribbons and posting missing person notices, with calls for the public to provide any leads.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Zelensky Seeks Progress in Geneva Talks Amid Concerns Over Concessions
data.description:
Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky has expressed hope for substantial progress in the upcoming trilateral talks between Ukraine, the United States, and Russia in Geneva, Switzerland this week. According to RTHK, Zelensky highlighted concerns over the demands for concessions from Ukraine during his speech at the Munich Security Conference.

Zelensky emphasized the importance of serious and meaningful negotiations that would benefit all parties involved. He accused Moscow of attempting to delay the negotiation process by changing its chief negotiator.

The Ukrainian leader acknowledged feeling pressure from U.S. President Donald Trump, noting that the issue of concessions is frequently raised by the American side. Zelensky expressed a desire to hear what compromises Moscow is willing to make, as Ukraine has already made significant concessions.

data.title: The kidnapping of Savannah's mother and the subsequent demand for a Bitcoin ransom have drawn nationwide attention in the United States.
data.description: A kidnapping case in Tucson, Arizona, has drawn national attention. Nancy Guthrie, mother of well-known morning talk show host Savannah Guthrie, disappeared from her home in the early hours of February 1st. Police believe she was forcibly taken. The case quickly became a media sensation, attracting a flood of media and online "amateur detectives," making Tucson a global focal point. Police stated that the suspect's identity has not yet been confirmed, and the investigation is ongoing. Reports indicate that the kidnappers allegedly demanded a ransom in Bitcoin anonymously, highlighting a new trend of extortion using crypto assets. A former FBI negotiator pointed out that although the suspect appeared masked on security camera footage, the high level of scrutiny and continued exposure makes it "difficult for them to remain hidden for long." The White House stated that the FBI will dedicate "all resources" to assisting the investigation. As the case continues to unfold, the local community has displayed yellow ribbons and posted missing person notices, appealing to the public for information.

data.title: SpaceX Adjusts Focus to Moon Development, Musk Clarifies Mars Commitment
data.description: SpaceX has reportedly shifted its focus from Mars to the Moon, aiming to establish a self-sustaining lunar city within the next decade. According to Jin10, this strategic pivot has sparked questions on social media regarding the rationale behind the change. In response, Elon Musk clarified that SpaceX remains committed to its Mars mission. He emphasized that the adjustment is not expected to delay the timeline for achieving a self-sustaining city on Mars by more than five years and could potentially accelerate the development process.

data.title: Traders Cautious as Market Tests 70K Resistance Level
data.description:
Crypto KOL 戈多Godot (@GodotSancho) posted on X. Traders are closely monitoring the market as it approaches the 70K resistance level again today. Yesterday, the price tested this resistance but fell back. Despite this, the 0.15 Delta Skew remains at a favorable 1.12, indicating a healthy pullback.

However, the skew for next week remains negative, with put options being more expensive. This suggests that traders are skeptical about the weekend's upward movement and are preparing for potential downturns next week. The previous high of 70,500 is a critical level, and while it might be tested again today, failure to break through could signal a return to a short-term bearish trend. In the medium to long term, the market remains in a bearish trend.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Munich Security Conference Concludes Amid Transatlantic Tensions
data.description: The 62nd Munich Security Conference concluded on February 15 in Munich, Germany. According to Jin10, the conference, which began in 1963 as the International Defense Conference focusing on transatlantic partnerships, highlighted growing tensions in transatlantic relations. Several European leaders emphasized the need for Europe to enhance its strategic autonomy.

data.description:

The remaining unswept levels sit below current price action, keeping the downside map technically incomplete. data.title: India's Central Bank Tightens Rules on Proprietary Trading Loans
data.description: India's central bank has implemented stricter regulations on loans for firms engaged in proprietary trading of shares and commodities. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this move is part of efforts to curb speculative market activities in the country. The new rules are designed to limit leverage offered to clients, aiming to stabilize the financial markets in India.

data.title: Garrett Jin's address deposited over 260,000 ETH into a CEX, worth approximately $543 million.
data.description: Lookonchain monitoring shows that the address of Garrett Jin, the agent of the "BTC OG insider whale", has started selling ETH again, and has just deposited 261,024 ETH (approximately $543 million) into Binance.

data.title: 10x Research: Cryptocurrency and related stocks are at a critical inflection point, with inflation falling faster than expected.
data.description: 10x Research published an article on its X platform stating that cryptocurrencies and related stocks are entering a critical inflection point, with inflation falling faster than expected and portfolio allocations reaching extreme levels. Despite weaker-than-expected earnings, Coinbase has quietly rebounded; and several mining companies shifting towards AI are attracting billions of dollars in institutional funding and long-term infrastructure investment. Bitcoin has stabilized after forced liquidations, with whales resuming accumulation, but key structural risks remain unresolved. Meanwhile, declining US Treasury yields and shifting market interest rate expectations are altering the macroeconomic environment. To optimize its capital structure and reduce direct equity dilution, MicroStrategy announced that future acquisitions will prioritize perpetual preferred stock over common stock. MSTR continues to face pressure, with unrealized losses reaching $4.8 billion.

data.title: X to Offer Cryptocurrency Trading Links, Not Brokerage Services
data.description: X is set to provide cryptocurrency trading links, according to ChainCatcher. Nikita Bier, the product lead at X, clarified that the platform will not execute trades or act as a broker. Instead, X will offer financial data and links to facilitate trading.
